draft_chunk.1: The Genealogy of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
draft_chunk.2: Abraham fathered Isaac, Isaac fathered Jacob, Jacob fathered Judah and his brothers.
draft_chunk.3: Judah fathered Perez and Zerah by Tamar, Perez fathered Hezron, Hezron fathered Ram.
draft_chunk.4: Ram fathered Amminadab, Amminadab fathered Nahshon, Nahshon fathered Salmon.
draft_chunk.5: Salmon fathered Boaz by Rahab, Boaz fathered Obed by Ruth, Obed fathered Jesse.
draft_chunk.6: Jesse fathered David the king. David fathered Solomon by the wife of Uriah.
draft_chunk.7: Solomon fathered Rehoboam, Rehoboam fathered Abijah, Abijah fathered Asa.
draft_chunk.8: Asa fathered Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat fathered Joram, Joram fathered Uzziah.
draft_chunk.9: Uzziah fathered Jotham, Jotham fathered Ahaz, Ahaz fathered Hezekiah.
draft_chunk.10: Hezekiah fathered Manasseh, Manasseh fathered Ammon, Ammon fathered Josiah.
draft_chunk.11: Josiah fathered Jeconiah and his brothers during the Babylonian exile.
draft_chunk.12: After the Babylonian exile, Jeconiah fathered Shealtiel, Shealtiel fathered Zerubbabel.
draft_chunk.13: Zerubbabel fathered Abiud, Abiud fathered Eliakim, Eliakim fathered Azor.
draft_chunk.14: Azor fathered Zadok, Zadok fathered Achim, Achim fathered Eliud.
draft_chunk.15: Eliud fathered Eleazar, Eleazar fathered Matthan, Matthan fathered Jacob.
draft_chunk.16: Jacob fathered Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom Jesus was born, who is called Christ.
draft_chunk.17: Thus from Abraham to David there were fourteen generations; from David to the Babylonian exile, fourteen generations; and from the Babylonian exile to Christ, fourteen generations.

editorial_input: This is a test sentence that should stay clear and complete.
editorial_output: Revised: This is a test sentence that should stay clear and complete.
